summary:
• Strategic Development: Tennessee State Parks is engaging the public to shape the future of a new state park at McDonald Farm, a significant landscape in Hamilton County. This initiative represents a key opportunity for community involvement in regional development.
  • Community Engagement: Arts Build is hosting its first Culture Works Happy Hour, fostering connections among businesses, artists, and arts nonprofits. This event aims to strengthen the local arts community and promote cultural collaboration.
  • Public Safety and Events: The Fire Department emphasizes safety during Independence Day celebrations, advocating for professional fireworks displays. Concurrently, Chattanooga is hosting a series of events to celebrate the July 4th week, enhancing community spirit and engagement.

items.title: 40% of people have confidence in their national government, according to the latest #OECDTrustData survey. Covering 33 #...
items.content_text:
40% of people have confidence in their national government, according to the latest #OECDTrustData survey.
 Covering 33 #OECD countries and five accession candidates, the survey helps governments track trust levels across institutions and pinpoints where reform is needed most.
 What do people say across OECD?
 ➡️ 40% trust national government
 ➡️ 46% trust local government
 ➡️ 42% believe decisions are evidence based
 ➡️ 68% believe voting makes a difference
 ➡️ 31% feel people like them have a real say in what government does.
 ➡️ 41% see potential in government use of AI
 The OECD Survey on Drivers of Trust in Public Institutions 2026 Results points to clear priorities for action: strengthening decision making on complex issues, fostering meaningful citizen participation, using technologies transparently and focusing on what drives trust in each institution.
 Find out more: https://brnw.ch/21x3JIm
 #BetterPoliciesForBetterLives

items.title: A new chapter is beginning for one of Hamilton County’s most significant landscapes. Tennessee State Parks is inviting t...
items.content_text:
A new chapter is beginning for one of Hamilton County’s most significant landscapes.
 Tennessee State Parks is inviting the public to help shape the future of a new state park at McDonald Farm. Approximately 1,300 acres of the farm will be transferred to the State of Tennessee, creating a new park that will eventually encompass about 2,500 acres and connect to the Cumberland Trail.
 This is an incredible opportunity to preserve a special place for future generations while creating new opportunities for recreation, conservation, and connection to our region’s history and natural resources.
 Community input will help guide the park’s master plan. Attend the open house on July 16 from 5–7 p.m. at the Harvest Barn at McDonald Farm (register below) or share your thoughts through the online survey: https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/TSP70PublicLink
 Register to attend via the the State of TN website: https://www.tn.gov/…/2…/6/26/tnsp-public-input-new-park.html #tennesseestateparks

items.title: Looking for plans to kick off the July 4th week? Chattanooga is bringing the heat with an incredible lineup of events! �...
items.content_text:
Looking for plans to kick off the July 4th week? Chattanooga is bringing the heat with an incredible lineup of events! 🎆 Here is your official guide to the City of Chattanooga and Hamilton County's BIG LINEUP: 
 ALL EVENTS ARE FREE.
 🎸 July 2: Pops on the River
 Where: Coolidge Park
 When: 4:00 PM – 11:00 PM
 The Vibe: Classic fireworks, live music, and family fun on the grass. Bring your lawn chairs!
 🎭 July 3: Hamilton in the Round
 Where: Renaissance Park
 When: 7:00 PM
 The Vibe: Join in for an epic "Hamilton" sing-along under the stars.
 🛸 July 4: United 250
 Where: Ross’s Landing
 We are celebrating 250 years of community and history with a massive bash! Expect Riverfront Nights live music, tons of food vendors, and a "Colonel Village" for family fun.
 The Grand Finale: A spectacular 500-drone show paired with a breathtaking pyrotechnic waterfall right off the Market Street Bridge!

items.title: Urban League of Greater Chattanooga has teamed up with Communities Speak to collect information on the state of mental h...
items.content_text:
Urban League of Greater Chattanooga has teamed up with Communities Speak to collect information on the state of mental health in Chattanooga. Make your voice heard by taking this anonymous survey here ➡️ https://sipacolumbia.co1.qualtrics.com/…/SV_54orqAsORcWGnVY… 
 Communities Speak works with community organizations through a survey distributed across participating partners. By gathering input collectively, we will develop a clearer picture of community needs, and work together to develop and implement solutions that improve mental health outcomes across the city.
 Share the link with friends, family, and others in your network. This survey is completely anonymous and takes under 10 minutes to complete. Participants can enter into a raffle to win a $40 gift card.
 Please visit the website cha.city/speak if you want to learn more about the initiative.

items.title: Although wildlife may appear cute, feeding them can be dangerous for both you and the animals. Animal bites are a common...
items.content_text:
Although wildlife may appear cute, feeding them can be dangerous for both you and the animals. Animal bites are a common source of serious infections and can occur through contact with an infected animal, a contaminated surface, or its habitat.
 Bites and scratches pose a significant risk of illness, including rabies, which primarily spreads through bites or scratches from infected wild animals such as raccoons, skunks, or foxes.
 To protect yourself, your family, and your pets, it's best to keep a safe distance from wildlife. For more information, please visit health.hamiltontn.org/…/se…/environmental/rabiescontrol.aspx or call (423) 209-8110.

items.content_text:
it’s important to remember that even after a storm moves through, hidden dangers can remain... especially around downed power lines.
 Always assume a line is energized and keep your distance. Stay at least 30 feet away, keep children and pets back, and report the hazard to EPB right away.
 Our crews are trained and ready to respond safely. Your job is simple: stay back and stay safe!

items.title: Incomes for farmers and other agricultural workers are projected to rise over the next decade, but progress will remain...
items.content_text:
Incomes for farmers and other agricultural workers are projected to rise over the next decade, but progress will remain uneven.
 While average global income per worker is projected to grow by 9% over the next decade, farmers in low-income regions of Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia still earn very little, with only modest gains ahead.
 Limited access to machinery, markets and higher-value products keeps agriculture worker incomes low. Boosting productivity, improving market access, and creating more rural job opportunities will be key to closing the gap and strengthening food security.
 Learn more about the state of agriculture today in latest #OECD-FAO Agricultural Outlook, launched today by OECD Secretary-General Mathias Cormann and FAO Director-General QU Dongyu: https://brnw.ch/21x3Jky
 #AgOutlook

items.title: We’re excited to introduce the 16 poets selected for Democracy Speaks, Chattanooga’s new civic poetry pathway at Renaiss...
items.content_text:
We’re excited to introduce the 16 poets selected for Democracy Speaks, Chattanooga’s new civic poetry pathway at Renaissance Park.
 Through a community selection process, these poets were chosen to create original works exploring democracy, history, civic engagement, and the many voices that make up our community. We are also proud that every poet participating in this project was compensated for their creative contributions.
 Selected poets and poems:
 • Amber Hickey – River Cache
 • Cheikh “CJ” Diakhate – Burdens
 • Cody Simon – Middle Ground
 • Desiree’ “Dezigner” Green – We Shall Overcome
 • Kelly “Stormy Poet” Williams – Chattanooga National Cemetery Speaks
 • Mirabelle Soufer – How Long
 • Philip Luckey – Garden Variety Democracy
 • Vander – Lacked Intolerance
 • Peggy Douglas – Abby Crawford Milton
 • Sylvia J. Greene – Eleanor and Mary
 • Kweens Crown – Rooted In Zion
 • Marcus Lee – Just A Song
 • Naomi Setliffe – Nineteenth is a Doorway
 • N’nako Kande’ – The Land Remembers
 • R. N. Letterman – Garden of Eden: The Watauga Longhunters and the Battle of Kings Mountain
 • Kenneth Allen Basham, III – America: A Nation of Mistaken Identity
 Thank you to our incredible partners at Rhyme N Chatt, who brought this vision to us and helped shape it into a meaningful public art experience. Special thanks to Project Manager Marsha Mills and community mentors and reviewers Laura Brown, Ian Seaton, and Linda Moss-Mines for their guidance, expertise, and thoughtful participation throughout the process.
 Join us on June 28 at Renaissance Park as we celebrate the opening of Democracy Speaks and hear these powerful voices come to life through poetry, community, and conversation.
 This Tennessee America 250 Program is supported in part by the Tennessee Commission for the United States Semiquincentennial.

Through a community selection process, these poets were chosen to create original works exploring democracy, history, civic engagement, and the many voices that make up our community. We are also proud that every poet participating in this project was compensated for their creative contributions.
Selected poets and poems:
• Amber Hickey – River Cache
• Cheikh “CJ” Diakhate – Burdens
• Cody Simon – Middle Ground
• Desiree’ “Dezigner” Green – We Shall Overcome
• Kelly “Stormy Poet” Williams – Chattanooga National Cemetery Speaks
• Mirabelle Soufer – How Long
• Philip Luckey – Garden Variety Democracy
• Vander – Lacked Intolerance
• Peggy Douglas – Abby Crawford Milton
• Sylvia J. Greene – Eleanor and Mary
• Kweens Crown – Rooted In Zion
• Marcus Lee – Just A Song
• Naomi Setliffe – Nineteenth is a Doorway
• N’nako Kande’ – The Land Remembers
• R. N. Letterman – Garden of Eden: The Watauga Longhunters and the Battle of Kings Mountain
• Kenneth Allen Basham, III – America: A Nation of Mistaken Identity
Thank you to our incredible partners at Rhyme N Chatt, who brought this vision to us and helped shape it into a meaningful public art experience. Special thanks to Project Manager Marsha Mills and community mentors and reviewers Laura Brown, Ian Seaton, and Linda Moss-Mines for their guidance, expertise, and thoughtful participation throughout the process.
Join us on June 28 at Renaissance Park as we celebrate the opening of Democracy Speaks and hear these powerful voices come to life through poetry, community, and conversation.
This Tennessee America 250 Program is supported in part by the Tennessee Commission for the United States Semiquincentennial.

items.title: Chattanooga Fire worked a motor vehicle crash with injuries and possible entrapment Saturday night on Hixson Pike at N A...
items.content_text:
Chattanooga Fire worked a motor vehicle crash with injuries and possible entrapment Saturday night on Hixson Pike at N Access Road involving two vehicles and multiple patients. 
 One vehicle was flipped on its top with one person trapped inside, unable to get out. There were a total of four passengers. The second vehicle had all parties out of the vehicle upon arrival. Firefighters worked together to free the victim from the wreckage. Once the patient was removed from the car, they were immediately placed in the ambulance for patient care. In total, three patients were transported to the hospital by HCEMS. CFD personnel cleaned up debris and placed oil dry where needed before returning to service.
 Engine 16, Squad 19, Squad 7 and Battalion 3 Red Shift responded. Photos and details from Captain Michael Battle. Crash investigations are conducted by CPD.

items.content_text:
In Asia, just 15% of listed companies disclose human capital information, such as employee turnover, health and safety training, workplace accidents, compared with 22% globally.
 Human capital management is becoming increasingly important, reflecting growing recognition of workforce‑related risks and opportunities.
 With investor and policymakers paying greater attention to issues such as talent, skills and employee well-being, how can countries improve reporting practices?
 Read the #OECDAsiaCapitalMarkets report to find out ➡️ https://brnw.ch/21x3Ib5
 #BetterPoliciesForBetterLives

items.title: Official development assistance (ODA) is projected to decline by 6.9% in 2026.  A new #OECD policy brief projects that n...
items.content_text:
Official development assistance (ODA) is projected to decline by 6.9% in 2026. 
 A new #OECD policy brief projects that net ODA could fall to its lowest level since 2014, marking a third consecutive year of decline. 
 Most of the expected reductions in 2026 come from a small number of the largest providers, although cuts are expected to be widespread across Development Assistance Committee (DAC) members. 
 The policy brief examines the implications for vulnerable countries and sectors, including: 
 ➡️ Further declines in bilateral ODA to sub-Saharan Africa and least developed countries 
 ➡️ Deep cuts to health, humanitarian aid and support to government and civil society 
 ➡️ A third consecutive year of decline in multilateral ODA, including reduced core funding to United Nations organisations 
 The policy brief suggests that the factors behind recent aid cuts are likely to persist, including fiscal consolidation, geopolitical tensions, increased spending on defence and security, and efforts to align public spending with domestic priorities. At the same time, needs in developing countries continue to grow. 
 Read the policy brief ➡️ https://brnw.ch/21x3HPX
 #ODA2026 #OECDdevelopment

items.content_text:
Shooting – Highway 153 South between Thrasher Bridge and Exit 5A
 The Chattanooga Police Department (CPD) is investigating a shooting that was reported to have occurred on Highway 153 South between Thrasher Bridge and Exit 5A. 
 At 4:40 A.M., CPD officers responded to reports of a person shot in the 4900 block of Bonny Oaks Drive. Upon arrival, officers learned the incident had not occurred at that location. The victim, a 15-year-old male, was taken to a local hospital by Hamilton County EMS with non-life-threatening injuries. 
 CPD Homicide and Crime Scene Units responded to investigate and canvas the area of the reported shooting location. The preliminary investigation indicates the victim was a passenger in a vehicle that was involved in a road rage incident with the suspect's vehicle. The investigation is ongoing and all leads are being thoroughly pursued. 
 This information is preliminary and subject to change as the investigation continues. Chattanooga Police ask anyone with information regarding this incident to call 423-698-2525.
 #CPDnews

Asian capital markets are growing but companies are not being valued much higher.
 Across the region, nearly 4 out of 10 companies in 2025 were valued by investors at less than what they’re worth on paper - much higher than in the rest of the world.
 Why is this happening and what can governments do to help companies use money more effectively, increase shareholder returns and make markets more competitive?
 Find out in the #OECDAsiaCapitalMarkets Report ➡️ https://brnw.ch/21x3HaY
 #BetterPoliciesForBetterLives

What should I do if ICE stops me in my car?🤔🚨
 We share our Know Your Rights video series with TIRRC. In this video we inform you about the rights you have if you are detained by ICE in your vehicle.
 Please stay calm, evaluate your situation and offer ONLY valid information. For more information and educational videos, visit our Resources for Immigrant Families page here -> https://www.lapazchattanooga.org/recursos 
 @followers
In this episode of Know Your Rights with Viri Marín from the Tennessee Immigrant and Refugee Rights Coalition [TIRRC] we talk about what we can do when an ICE agent stops us in our vehicle.
 In this video, we talk about the rights you have when you are stopped in your car and how important it is to evaluate and understand the situation you are in. Please be sure to save your car information and provide only valid information. 🌿

items.title: Today marks National PTSD Awareness Day, so let's talk about this devastating mental health condition. Did you know that...
items.content_text:
Today marks National PTSD Awareness Day, so let's talk about this devastating mental health condition.
 Did you know that about 6% of U.S. adults will experience PTSD at some point in their lives? The rate is 10 -20% for first responders due to their repeated exposure to traumatic events throughout their careers. The good news is that a variety of treatment options are available. But first, you must recognize the symptoms. They include flashbacks, sleep disturbances, hypervigilance, irritability, emotional numbness, and difficulty concentrating.
 Did you know that about 6% of U.S. adults will experience PTSD at some point in their lives? That rate is significantly higher for first responders, who are repeatedly exposed to traumatic events throughout their careers. Studies estimate that 10-20% of first responders may experience PTSD symptoms during their careers. The good news is that a variety of treatment options are available. But first, you must recognize the symptoms. They include flashbacks, sleep disturbances, hypervigilance, irritability, emotional numbness, and difficulty concentrating.
 CPD has prioritized employee wellbeing by creating a dedicated Wellness Unit. It includes a proactive Peer Support Program that launched in 2016, a Peer Support Mentor for cadets, wellness training focused on resilience, stress management, and trauma awareness, resources for employees and their families, and efforts to reduce stigma around mental health.
 Remember, if you or a loved one are in crisis you can call or text 988 anytime.

items.content_text:
When Samantha Dean crossed the stage at the UTC May commencement, she did so after building a graduate school experience rooted in psychology, research and a growing sense of where her career was headed. 👏 
 Soon after earning her master’s degree in psychological studies, Dean moved into a full-time role as a pre-award coordinator in UTC’s Office of Research and Sponsored Programs.
 “I grew up always focusing on other people and how they’re feeling and whatever, so now I’ve kind of transitioned into what I want, what I would actually enjoy, and what would make me happy going forward.”
 🔗  Read more here: https://blog.utc.edu/…/research-psychology-and-purpose-sha…/

items.title: Thanks to the nearly 50 volunteers from Alive in You Service Camp for all your help at Champion's Club and Rivermont Par...
items.content_text:
Thanks to the nearly 50 volunteers from Alive in You Service Camp for all your help at Champion's Club and Rivermont Park! 
 These incredible kids helped tend to garden beds, clear out debris from last week's wild storm, picked up litter, trimmed bushes, and spread thousands of pounds of mulch at the Rivermont playground. They got so much accomplished in just a few short hours! 
 Volunteer groups like this are instrumental to our work. Interested in lending a hand? We would love to have you! Email our Volunteer and Community Engagement Coordinator, Austin Adderholt, at aadderholt@chattanooga.gov.

items.title: Keeping Illegal Drugs and Firearms Off the Streets Two separate CPD narcotics investigations concluded this week with th...
items.content_text:
Keeping Illegal Drugs and Firearms Off the Streets
 Two separate CPD narcotics investigations concluded this week with the arrest of three men on multiple drug and weapons charges and the seizure of a substantial amount of illegal drugs and firearms. 
 The first, a long-term CPD narcotics unit investigation involving months of investigative work, assistance from CPD’s Gun Team and Neighborhood Policing officers, a traffic stop, and a search warrant resulted in two arrests and the seizure of nearly 12 lbs of meth, more than 300 grams of fentanyl, ecstasy, oxycodone pills, eight handguns (two stolen), a vehicle, and a substantial amount of drug paraphernalia. 
 The second, a stop of the suspect and subsequent search warrants, led to the arrest of the suspect and the seizure of more than two kilos of cocaine, over a pound of fentanyl, meth, two firearms, a significant amount of cash, a brick press, and more drug paraphernalia. 
 All three suspects are in custody at the Hamilton County Jail.

items.title: Chattanooga! We've got some hot weather headed our way next week! Let’s keep cool and cut energy costs. When the heat in...
items.content_text:
Chattanooga! We've got some hot weather headed our way next week! Let’s keep cool and cut energy costs. When the heat index climbs, try these simple tips to stay comfortable and save energy:
 - Keep blinds and curtains closed to block out the sun
 - Use fans to help circulate air (set ceiling fans to spin counterclockwise)
 - Raise your thermostat a few degrees when you're not home
 - Skip the oven and dryer during peak heat hours
 - Check for air leaks around windows and doors
 Small changes can make a big impact!

items.title: Career Opportunity in #EastRidgeTN! 🚧 The City of East Ridge is seeking a motivated and dependable individual to join o...
items.content_text:
Career Opportunity in #EastRidgeTN! 🚧
 The City of East Ridge is seeking a motivated and dependable individual to join our Street Department team as a full-time Equipment Operator!
 In this role, you'll help maintain and improve our community through a variety of duties including street maintenance, construction projects, mowing, stormwater maintenance, and operating a variety of equipment and vehicles.
 Qualifications:
 ✔️ Must be at least 18 years of age
 ✔️ Valid Driver's License required
 ✔️ High School Diploma or equivalent
 Preferred Experience:
 👏🏼 Stormwater, asphalt, concrete, and mowing operations
 👏🏼 Operating equipment such as a backhoe, mini excavator, and skid steer
 This position requires after-hours availability for emergency calls and the ability to lift up to 50 pounds.
 Join a team dedicated to serving the residents of East Ridge and helping keep our city moving forward!
 💻 Apply online today at www.eastridgetn.gov/human_resources.html
 The City of East Ridge is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer and a Tennessee Drug-Free Workplace.

Europe’s current heatwave is a reminder that extreme heat is not only a climate issue. It is a child health, development and urban...
items.content_text:
Europe’s current heatwave is a reminder that extreme heat is not only a climate issue. It is a child health, development and urban policy issue.

Young children are especially vulnerable to heat stress, with risks to their health, development, learning and wellbeing beginning before birth.

In this open-access online masterclass, Dr Lindsey Burghardt, Chief Science Officer at the Center on the Developing Child at Harvard University, speaks with Katie Beck, Policy Fellow at LSE Cities, about the impacts of extreme heat on children’s developing bodies and minds, and the urban policy responses that can help mitigate harm.

The session was presented by the Van Leer Foundation, the Urban95 Academy at LSE and the Center on the Developing Child at Harvard University and is now available free to a wider audience.

📽️ Watch the recording here: https://lnkd.in/eEURdHKD

#ExtremeHeat #Heatwave #UrbanHeat #ChildHealth #EarlyChildhoodDevelopment #UrbanPolicy #ClimateAdaptation #ChildFriendlyCities #Urban95Academy

items.title: Sharing an opportunity for muralists from  AIM Center, Inc.!Call for Professional Mural Artists! 🎨 The AIM Center invit...
items.content_text:
Sharing an opportunity for muralists from  AIM Center, Inc.!
Call for Professional Mural Artists! 🎨
 The AIM Center invites qualified mural artists and artist teams to submit qualifications for the design and installation of a large-scale exterior mural at Espero Chattanooga, a new permanent supportive housing development located at the corner of Main Street and Hawthorne Avenue in Chattanooga's historic Highland Park neighborhood.
 The selected artist will co-create directly with AIM Center members, adults living with serious mental illness (SMI), to develop a mural concept that reflects the spirit of Espero, which means "hope" in Spanish. The artwork should celebrate themes of hope, belonging, recovery, resilience, community, and the transformative impact of safe, affordable housing.
 Artists from Chattanooga and the surrounding Appalachian region (TN, GA,AL), especially those with lived experience of mental illness, are strongly encouraged to apply.
 🗓 Applications must be submitted by Friday, July 24, 2026, at 4:00 PM EST.
 Review the complete Request for Qualifications (RFQ), eligibility requirements, project timeline, budget, and submission instructions here ➡ https://aimcenterinc.org/…/espero-chattanooga-mural-project/
 Questions regarding the Espero Chattanooga Mural Project, artist eligibility, submission requirements, or the RFQ process may be directed to development@aimcenterinc.org.

items.title: Former Red Bank Officer Indicted for Operating Patrol Vehicle Intoxicated Hamilton County, TN
items.content_text:
Former Red Bank Officer Indicted for Operating Patrol Vehicle Intoxicated
 Hamilton County, TN – Hamilton County Sheriff Austin Garrett and Hamilton County District Attorney General Coty Wamp announce the grand jury indictment of former Red Bank Police Officer Brantley Young for Driving Under the Influence and Possession of a Firearm While Under the Influence.
 A joint investigation by the HCSO Criminal Investigations Unit and Traffic Investigations Unit into a January 2026 incident involving a suspicion that Young was operating a patrol vehicle under the influence resulted in grand jurors indicting Young. Young was booked into the Hamilton County Jail & Detention Center early this morning.

🗓 Applications must be submitted by Friday, July 24, 2026, at 4:00 PM EST.
Review the complete Request for Qualifications (RFQ), eligibility requirements, project timeline, budget, and submission instructions here ➡ https://aimcenterinc.org/…/espero-chattanooga-mural-project/
Questions regarding the Espero Chattanooga Mural Project, artist eligibility, submission requirements, or the RFQ process may be directed to development@aimcenterinc.org.

items.title: Slovenia’s economy has demonstrated resilience amid external shocks, with its real GDP growing at a rate surpassing that...
items.content_text:
Slovenia’s economy has demonstrated resilience amid external shocks, with its real GDP growing at a rate surpassing that of the OECD and the euro area since 2022.
 Projected GDP growth is set to strengthen to 1.9% in 2026 and 2.2% in 2027, as energy price pressures are assumed to diminish. 
 Inflation is expected to gradually ease by 2027.
 For further insights, refer to the #OECD Economic Survey of Slovenia ➡️ https://brnw.ch/21x3Fcv

items.title: OMI is scheduled for Wednesday and Thursday, Aug. 19-20, in residential spaces across campus as new students move in. Th...
items.content_text:
OMI is scheduled for Wednesday and Thursday, Aug. 19-20, in residential spaces across campus as new students move in. The two-day effort brings together faculty, staff, students and organizations to create a welcoming, supportive environment for incoming students and their families. 💙 
 "At OMI, there is no performance. There are parents trying to keep things moving. There are grandparents cheering from the sidewalk. There are first-generation college students stepping into a new world with family members who may be navigating a college campus for the first time. There are parents dropping off a fourth child and parents dropping off their only child.”
 🔗  Learn more here: https://blog.utc.edu/…/people-are-the-thing-operation-move…/

items.title: This grass at Miller Park will look a little blue this October! So excited to host IBMA again this year. One of the many...
items.content_text:
This grass at Miller Park will look a little blue this October! So excited to host IBMA again this year. One of the many things that makes our city a great place to live.
Say hello to Pickin’ in the Park 🎶🌿
 Formerly known as Streetfest, this fan-favorite free festival experience is coming back to IBMA Bluegrass Live!® in 2026, now with a fresh name and a new home in Chattanooga's Miller Park.
 Join us October 23 and 24 for two days of live bluegrass from college bands, Kids on Bluegrass, and local Chattanooga talent, plus arts and crafts vendors, food, and plenty of space to kick back and enjoy the music. No fences, no tickets, just a community gathering to celebrate bluegrass together. 💛
 While Pickin’ in the Park is free and open to all, tickets will be required for the IBMA Bluegrass Live!® indoor performances, and they'll be on sale soon at worldofbluegrass.org. 👀
 Speaking of IBMA Bluegrass Live!®… a quick reminder that 2026 is bringing a new vibe. 🙌 This year’s festival will offer a more intimate, curated experience in the heart of Chattanooga, with main stages at the Chattanooga Convention Center, The Signal, and Barrelhouse Ballroom. Expect a closer connection to the music you love, with a limited number of tickets available.
 More details coming soon. For now, mark your calendars and get ready for an awesome weekend. 🎻
 Photo: Corey Pelton
 #IBMA #IBMA2026 #IBMAWorldofBluegrass #VisitChatt Visit Chattanooga

items.title: 🧡 June is Gun Violence Awareness Month. By the end of today, two women in America will likely be shot and killed by a c...
items.content_text:
🧡 June is Gun Violence Awareness Month.
 By the end of today, two women in America will likely be shot and killed by a current or former intimate partner.
 This is more than a statistic.
 It's two lives cut short.
 Two families forever changed.
 Two communities left grieving.
 Research shows that when an abusive partner has access to a firearm, the risk that a woman will be killed increases dramatically.
 Domestic violence and gun violence are not separate issues. They intersect every day in homes and communities across our country.
 This month, we honor those whose lives have been lost, support survivors, and recommit ourselves to preventing violence before it turns deadly.
 Because safety should never depend on whether an abuser has access to a gun.
 #GunViolenceAwarenessMonth #WearOrange #EndDomesticViolence #DomesticViolenceAwareness #PreventionMatters #TNCoalition #EndTheViolence

items.title: Join us this Sunday, June 28, for CareFest at Shredder's Lair—an all-day market, concert, and community fundraiser benef...
items.content_text:
Join us this Sunday, June 28, for CareFest at Shredder's Lair—an all-day market, concert, and community fundraiser benefiting the Comunidad Care Fund.
 By attending, you'll make a tangible difference for families impacted by immigration enforcement, connect with local organizers and entrepreneurs, discover ways to get involved and enjoy a full day of music, food, art, and community.
 🎵 11 AM–3 PM: Vendor market & free live music
 📢 3–4 PM: Community program on the immigrant justice landscape in Chattanooga and Hamilton County
 🎸 5–10 PM: Rock benefit concert ($10 at the door, with 100% of ticket sales supporting the Care Fund)
 CareFest is built on a simple belief: when systems criminalize and abandon our neighbors, we show up for one another.
 That's why musicians, artists, vendors, volunteers, and local businesses are donating their time, talent and resources to support families in our community.
 Bring a friend. Support local vendors & artists. Meet your neighbors. Help us raise $5,000 for the Comunidad Care Fund.

items.title: HCSO Arrests Man after Vehicle Pursuit with Multiple Warrants and Illegal Items Hamilton County, TN
items.content_text:
HCSO Arrests Man after Vehicle Pursuit with Multiple Warrants and Illegal Items
 Hamilton County, TN – Early this morning on June 25, 2026, an alert was sent out for a stolen vehicle near the intersection of Campbell Street and Glass Street.
 A Chattanooga Police Department officer located the vehicle, and once an HCSO deputy arrived as backup, initiated a traffic stop. The driver fled, initiating a vehicle pursuit that was taken over by the HCSO deputy. The pursuit ended when the vehicle crashed at the 2300 block of East 5th Street.
 The driver, Thomas Williams, was taken into custody by the HCSO, and deputies searched the vehicle, resulting in the seizure of the following:
 • 1 ounce of Methamphetamine
 • 7 ounces of Cocaine
 • One glass pipe
 • $944 in cash
 Williams faces the following charges: 
 • Evading Arrest
 • Reckless Endangerment
 • Theft of Property 
 • Driving While in Possession of Methamphetamine 
 • Drugs General Category for Resale
 • Possession of Controlled Substance
 • Possession of Drug Paraphernalia
 In addition to this incident, Williams was found to have outstanding warrants from the Hamilton County Criminal Court, Hamilton County General Sessions Court, and the East Ridge Police Department.
 This information is preliminary and subject to change. No further details are available.

We’re proud to share that Emily Mack, President & CEO of River City Company, has been selected for Class XIII of Leadership Tennessee’s Signature Program!
 This prestigious program brings together accomplished leaders from across the state to explore Tennessee’s most pressing challenges, engage in meaningful dialogue, and collaborate on solutions that create lasting impact for communities across Tennessee. 
 Emily’s selection reflects her commitment to strong leadership, community development, and advancing opportunities for Chattanooga and the region. We’re honored to see her recognized alongside such an outstanding group of leaders and look forward to following her journey and contributions with this year’s cohort.

🌎🌊 Water Fest is back—with a new date! 🌎🌊
 Mark your calendars for Friday, July 10th from 4:00–7:00 PM and join us at the Red Bank High School Track and Field for an afternoon of family-friendly fun and community connection!
 Enjoy bounce houses, face painting, and interactive activities while learning more about the waterways that make our region so special. Local organizations will be on-site to share how we can all help protect our aquatic environment.

 Bring the whole family and celebrate water, community, and summer with us—we hope to see you there! 💧
 *Admission is FREE!

items.title: The new #OECD Economic Survey of the Slovak Republic highlights the need to strengthen public finances while implementin...
items.content_text:
The new #OECD Economic Survey of the Slovak Republic highlights the need to strengthen public finances while implementing structural reforms to enhance business dynamism and long-term growth.
 The Slovak economy is facing growing pressure from global trade tensions, a weak European car market, a large budget deficit, and its ageing population.
 The Slovak Republic needs to reform retirement rules to extend working lives and encourage female labour market participation through childcare and parental leave reforms.
 Additionally, stronger R&D support and faster AI adoption would boost labour productivity and diversify the car manufacturing base into emerging areas of demand. Reducing regulatory barriers, investing in skills, and accelerating the digital and green transitions are essential for achieving sustainable, inclusive growth.
 Read more: https://brnw.ch/21x3DSw

What an incredible week for our student artists! Last week’s showcase at the Hamilton County Courthouse was a beautiful celebration of creativity and community.
 We want to send a heartfelt thank you to the Hamilton County Courthouse officials for hosting our students and supporting this exhibition. We are also deeply grateful to the families, educators, ArtsBuild board members, and community advocates who came out to walk the gallery and champion these rising talents.
 🖼️ Missed the courthouse exhibit? You're in luck!
 The showcase has officially moved to its next stop. You can view the artwork at Chattanooga State’s Heinley Arts Center from now through July 30th.
 Join us for the grand finale! We will be hosting a Closing Showcase & Artist Reception at Chatt State on Thursday, July 30, from 6:00 p.m. to 7:00 p.m. Come celebrate the students one last time—plus, artists will be able to take their artwork home with them right after the closing ceremony.
 Check out some moments from last week's courthouse exhibit below! 👇📸
 #ArtsBuild #ChattanoogaState #ChattArts #ArtsEducation #CommunityPartnerships #StudentShowcase

Join us for the grand finale! We will be hosting a Closing Showcase & Artist Reception at Chatt State on Thursday, July 30, from 6:00 p.m. to 7:00 p.m. Come celebrate the students one last time—plus, artists will be able to take their artwork home with them right after the closing ceremony.
Check out some moments from last week's courthouse exhibit below! 👇📸

items.title: A huge thank you to our EPB crews and the additional utility workers who worked continuously day and night to restore po...
items.content_text:
A huge thank you to our EPB crews and the additional utility workers who worked continuously day and night to restore power following Monday night's severe storms.
 From repairing broken poles and replacing downed lines to navigating difficult conditions after dark, these teams have worked tirelessly to safely restore service to our community. Thanks to their dedication and the support of crews from neighboring utilities, thousands of customers have already had their power restored.
 We're grateful for the hard work, long hours, and commitment these crews show when our community needs them most. We're also incredibly grateful to our customers for your support throughout the restoration process. 💙
